title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 32 points with eight assists, leading the Celtics to a 114-99 victory over the Hornets.
Payton Pritchard excelled in the starting lineup, contributing 28 points and six assists against Charlotte.
The Celtics exploited defensive mismatches, particularly targeting LaMelo Ball, as they aim for the 2-seed in the playoffs.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics secured a 50th win and clinched a playoff berth by defeating Charlotte Hornets, 114-99.
This marks Boston's 12th consecutive postseason appearance, maintaining the longest-active playoff streak in the NBA.
Jaylen Brown praised the team's success on social media despite sitting out the game due to Achilles tendinitis.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early achieves 2.19 ERA in first five career starts with Boston Red Sox.
Early pitched 5.1 innings, allowing one run against the Reds in a 3-2 loss.
Initially outside the starting rotation, Early joined due to other pitchers' unavailability.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet clinched a Walter Cup Playoffs spot after a 4-2 win over Minnesota Frost.
The Fleet, the first team to secure a playoff spot, boast a 13-5-2-4 record.
Rookie goalie Amanda Thiele made 21 saves in her PWHL debut for Boston.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter in fatal South Boston stabbing, police reported.
Police responded to 258 Old Colony Ave., where a man was fatally stabbed and later died in the hospital.
Charges against Pascual were upgraded to manslaughter after the victim's death; arraignment set for South Boston District Court.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police shot a 44-year-old man in the shoulder after he allegedly fired at officers during a pursuit.
A 71-year-old man sleeping nearby was grazed by a bullet in the exchange and both were hospitalized with non-life threatening injuries.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident, and a handgun with an obliterated serial number was recovered.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
John Tortorella, a Stanley Cup champion coach, will replace Cassidy as the head coach of the Golden Knights.
Vegas struggled with a recent losing streak and poor goalie performances despite being third in the Pacific Division.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are projected to draft Clemson DE T.J. Parker with the 31st pick according to NFL.com's mock draft.
Parker, with 21.5 sacks over three seasons at Clemson, is seen as a high-upside pass-rusher for the Patriots.
New England signed Dre’Mont Jones but needs further pass-rushing help after K’Lavon Chaisson joined the Commanders.

title: North Shore police department asking for public’s help after theft of historical plaques
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Newburyport police seek public assistance after a historical plaque was stolen from Old Hill graveyard on March 24.
The plaque marked a historic African American section, honoring those buried in the 18th and 19th centuries.
Police, lacking surveillance footage or witnesses, link this theft to several other recent bronze plaque thefts in the area.

title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed Merrimack College goalie Max Lundgren to a one-year entry-level contract for $952,500.
Lundgren, 23, posted a 21-16-2 record with a 2.55 goals-against average and a .920 save percentage in 2025-26.
The Swedish goalie earned Hockey East tournament MVP honors, helping Merrimack win its first championship in program history.
